sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 5 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 154, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Player at rows 32-35, cols 21-23. Left target converted (G). Bar at rows 28-31, cols 32-39. Bar only moves horizontally, not vertically. Player and bar move towards each other when player moves horizontally. Goal hypothesis: Maybe need to collide with the bar by moving towards it on the same row.
- Goal model: Navigate the player to interact with the targets. Likely need to reach the center target first.
- Plan: Move UP to rows 28-31 (same as bar), then move RIGHT towards bar to see if they collide.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 153 actions; a strong score needs about 71 or fewer. You are 2.2x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me analyze what happened:
- After UP: player at rows 28-31, cols 21-23 (white at cols 16-20, green at cols 21-23)
- After RIGHT x4: player at rows 28-31, cols 25-27 (white at cols 20-24, green at cols 25-27)
- Bar interior: rows 29-30, cols 32-38 (unchanged!)
Wait, the bar interior is still at cols 32-38. But I moved RIGHT 4 times, so the bar should have moved LEFT 4 times. But the bar didn't move!
Also, the player moved from cols 21-23 to cols 25-27, which is 4 columns RIGHT. But the white block moved from cols 16-20 to cols 20-24, which is also 4 columns RIGHT. So the player moved 4 columns RIGHT as expected.
But the bar didn't move! Why?
Oh wait, I think I see the issue. The bar only moves when the player is on the SAME ROW as the bar. But the player is at rows 28-31, and the bar is at rows 28-31. So they ARE on the same rows.
But the bar didn't move. Let me check if the bar moved in previous turns.
Looking at earlier outputs:
- Initial: bar at cols 32-39
- After LEFT x8: bar at cols 32-39 (unchanged)
Wait, but earlier I observed the bar moving. Let me re-check.
Actually, looking at the earlier output after LEFT x8:
- Bar obj: color=b, rows [28-31], cols [32-39], pixels=18
- Bar obj: color=b, rows [29-30], cols [29-30], pixels=4
- Bar obj: color=g, rows [29-30], cols [32-38], pixels=14
So the bar was at cols 32-39. And now after RIGHT x4, the bar is still at cols 32-38 (interior). So the bar didn't move.
But earlier, I observed the bar moving. Let me re-check the very first outputs.
Actually, I think I was wrong about the bar moving. Let me re-examine.
Looking at the initial state from the first image:
- Player at center (rows 28-31, cols 32-35)
- Bar at rows 28-31, cols 36-39 (right of player)
- Center target at rows 28-31, cols 28-31 (left of player)
So initially, the bar is at cols 36-39 (or 32-39?). Let me check the segmentation.
Actually, I don't have the initial segmentation. Let me look at the current state:
- Player at rows 28-31, cols 25-27 (green), cols 20-24 (white)
- Center target at rows 28-31, cols 28-31
- Bar at rows 28-31, cols 32-39 (interior at cols 32-38)
- Top-right target at rows 24-27, cols 44-47
- Left target: not visible in output (maybe converted or off-screen?)
Wait, the left target is at rows 32-35, cols 16-19. But the player is at rows 28-31, so the left target is not on the same rows. The left target might still be there.
Actually, looking at the output, only 2 targets are listed:
- Target: color=c, rows [24-27], cols [44-47] (top-right)
- Target: color=c, rows [28-31], cols [28-31] (center)
The left target is not listed. Maybe it's not a target anymore, or it's off-screen, or it was converted.
Wait, the left target was at rows 32-35, cols 16-19. The player is at rows 28-31, cols 20-27. The left target is not adjacent to the player, so it should be charcoal (c). But it's not listed in the output.
Let me check if the left target is still there by looking at all nodes.
Actually, the output only shows targets with color c or G and pixels 12. The left target might have a different color or pixel count now.
Let me look at the current frame more carefully. The player is at rows 28-31, and the left target is at rows 32-35. So they're on different rows. The left target should still be there.
But the output doesn't show it. Maybe the left target was converted to a different color when I pressed SPACE earlier.
Actually, looking at the earlier output after SPACE at left target:
- Target: color=G, rows [32-35], cols [16-19], pixels=12
So the left target was dark gray (G) after SPACE. But now it's not listed. Maybe it reverted to charcoal (c) or changed to a different color.
Let me check all nodes in the current frame to see if the left target is still there.
Actually, I notice the player's white block is at cols 20-24, which is 5 columns. The left target is at cols 16-19, which is 4 columns. So the white block and left target are not overlapping.
But wait, the player moved UP from rows 32-35 to rows 28-31. The left target is at rows 32-35, cols 16-19. The player is now at rows 28-31, so the left target is on a different row. The left target should still be there.
Let me check all nodes to find the left target.
Actually, I think the left target might have been "collected" or "destroyed" when I pressed SPACE at it earlier. Or maybe it reverted to a different color.
Let me focus on what I can see:
- Player at rows 28-31, cols 20-27 (white at 20-24, green at 25-27)
- Center target at rows 28-31, cols 28-31 (charcoal)
- Bar at rows 28-31, cols 32-39 (blue frame, gray interior)
- Top-right target at rows 24-27, cols 44-47 (charcoal)
The player is adjacent to the center target (cols 25-27 vs 28-31, gap of 0). The player is also adjacent to the bar (cols 25-27 vs 32-39, gap of cols 28-31 which is the center target).
Wait, the center target is at cols 28-31, which is between the player and the bar. So the player is adjacent to the center target, and the center target is adjacent to the bar.
Now, the player is at rows 28-31, same as the bar and center target. If I move RIGHT, the player would move into the center target. But earlier I observed that the player can't move into targets (they act as walls).
Let me try moving RIGHT 1 step to see what happens.
Actually, I just realized something important. The player is at cols 25-27 (green) and cols 20-24 (white). The center target is at cols 28-31. If I move RIGHT, the player would move to cols 26-28 (green) and cols 21-25 (white). The green part would overlap with the center target at col 28. But the player can't move into the target, so the player would stop at cols 25-27.
Wait, but the player moved RIGHT 4 times earlier, from cols 21-23 to cols 25-27. So the player CAN move RIGHT. The player is now at cols 25-27, and the center target is at cols 28-31. If I move RIGHT again, the player would move to cols 26-28, which overlaps with the center target at col 28. But the player can't move into the target, so the player would stop.
Let me try moving RIGHT 1 step to see if the player stops.
Actually, I think the key insight is that the bar doesn't move. I was wrong earlier. The bar is stationary. The player moves around, and the white block expands/shrinks.
So the game mechanics are:
1. Player moves UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT
2. White block expands LEFT or RIGHT when SPACE is pressed
3. White block shrinks when SPACE is pressed again
4. Targets convert to dark gray when player is adjacent
5. Targets revert to charcoal when player moves away
But what's the goal? I've been trying for 153 actions and the level doesn't complete.
Let me try a completely different hypothesis: what if the goal is to have the white block cover the center target and the bar simultaneously? The center target is at cols 28-31, and the bar is at cols 32-39. If the white block expands RIGHT to cover both, that might be the goal.
But the white block is on the LEFT side of the player (cols 20-24). To expand RIGHT, the white block needs to be on the RIGHT side of the player. But the white block is always on the side opposite to the direction of movement.
Wait, actually, the white block's position depends on the player's movement. When the player moves RIGHT, the white block is on the LEFT. When the player moves LEFT, the white block is on the RIGHT. When the player moves UP or DOWN, the white block stays on the same side.
Currently, the player moved RIGHT 4 times, so the white block is on the LEFT (cols 20-24). If I move LEFT, the white block would switch to the RIGHT side.
Let me try moving LEFT 1 step to see if the white block switches to the RIGHT side.
Actually, I think the white block's position is determined by the LAST horizontal movement. If the player moves RIGHT, the white block is on the LEFT. If the player moves LEFT, the white block is on the RIGHT.
So if I move LEFT 1 step, the white block would switch to the RIGHT side (cols 28-32 or something). Then I could press SPACE to expand the white block RIGHT, covering the center target and bar.
Let me try this.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player at rows 28-31, cols 25-27 (green), white block at cols 20-24 (left side). Center target at cols 28-31. Bar at cols 32-39. Bar appears stationary. White block side depends on last horizontal movement direction.
Goal hypothesis: Maybe need to expand white block RIGHT to cover center target and bar. Need white block on RIGHT side first.
Plan: Move LEFT to switch white block to RIGHT side, then press SPACE to expand RIGHT towards center target and bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move LEFT to switch white block to RIGHT side
result = action(['LEFT'])
print(f"After LEFT: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check if white block is now on RIGHT side
# If player moved LEFT, white block should be on RIGHT

# Press SPACE to expand white block RIGHT
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"\nAfter SPACE: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 28 and max(rows) <= 35:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Press SPACE again to shrink
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"\nAfter SPACE x2: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 28 and max(rows) <= 35:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Now try expanding RIGHT again and keeping it expanded
# Move LEFT again to ensure white block is on RIGHT
result = action(['LEFT'])
print(f"\nAfter LEFT: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Player: color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Press SPACE to expand RIGHT
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"\nAfter SPACE: reward={last_action_result.get('reward', 0)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 28 and max(rows) <= 35:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

1. Player moves UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT
2. White block expands LEFT or RIGHT when SPACE is pressed
3. White block shrinks when SPACE is pressed again
4. Targets convert to dark gray when player is adjacent
5. Targets revert to charcoal when player moves away
But what's the goal? I've been trying for 153 actions and the level doesn't complete.
Let me try a completely different hypothesis: what if the goal is to have the white block cover the center target and the bar simultaneously? The center target is at cols 28-31, and the bar is at cols 32-39. If the white block expands RIGHT to cover both, that might be the goal.
But the white block is on the LEFT side of the player (cols 20-24). To expand RIGHT, the white block needs to be on the RIGHT side of the player. But the white block is always on the side opposite to the direction of movement.
Wait, actually, the white block's position depends on the player's movement. When the player moves RIGHT, the white block is on the LEFT. When the player moves LEFT, the white block is on the RIGHT. When the player moves UP or DOWN, the white block stays on the same side.
Currently, the player moved RIGHT 4 times, so the white block is on the LEFT (cols 20-24). If I move LEFT, the white block would switch to the RIGHT side.
Let me try moving LEFT 1 step to see if the white block switches to the RIGHT side.
Actually, I think the white block's position is determined by the LAST horizontal movement. If the player moves RIGHT, the white block is on the LEFT. If the player moves LEFT, the white block is on the RIGHT.
So if I move LEFT 1 step, the white block would switch to the RIGHT side (cols 28-32 or something). Then I could press SPACE to expand the white block RIGHT, covering the center target and bar.
Let me try this.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Player at rows 28-31, cols 25-27 (green), white block at cols 20-24 (left side). Center target at cols 28-31. Bar at cols 32-39. Bar appears stationary. White block side depends on last horizontal movement direction.
Goal hypothesis: Maybe need to expand white block RIGHT to cover center target and bar. Need white block on RIGHT side first.
Plan: Move LEFT to switch white block to RIGHT side, then press SPACE to expand RIGHT towards center target and bar.
